U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Check calendar for scheduled meetings
Search calendar (via calendar_search_events or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to) for events related to the leadership meeting or pricing model review. Discover the calendar is completely empty — neither meeting has been formally scheduled.
OE 2: Search emails and Slack for meeting details and attendees
Search emails and Slack #executive channel for references to "leadership meeting" and "pricing model review." Discover Elena's email (Re: Root Cause Analysis: March AWS Cost Spike) references a Monday leadership meeting with recipients Marcus Thorne, Samira Tariq, Julian Brooks, Catalina Dubois, Lena Björkström, and Alejandro Fuentes. Elena asked Marcus to arrange a separate pricing model review meeting with Samira, Julian, and Catalina — no confirmation from Marcus exists.
OE 3: Create calendar event for Leadership Meeting
Create a calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) titled with a reference to the leadership meeting, including attendees from Elena's email thread (Marcus Thorne, Samira Tariq, Julian Brooks, Catalina Dubois, Lena Björkström, Alejandro Fuentes, and Elena Rostova). Topic covers AWS cost spike, pricing model, and infrastructure planning.
OE 4: Search for Marcus's pricing review confirmation
Search emails and Slack for any message from Marcus Thorne confirming he arranged or scheduled the pricing model review meeting per Elena's directive. Discover no such confirmation exists in any channel.
OE 5: Send follow-up to Marcus Thorne about pricing review
Send a Slack message (via conversations_add_message) to Marcus Thorne asking whether he has arranged the pricing model review meeting with Samira, Julian, and Catalina, and requesting a confirmed date so calendar invites can be sent.
OE 6: Search for evidence of Mina's Vectral proposal
Search emails for messages from Mina Hashimoto to Rachel Whitfield at Vectral Systems. Discover Mina sent the 10% Preferred Partner counter-proposal, Rachel counter-proposed 12%, Mina held at 10% with three service sweeteners (dedicated onboarding, 48-hour SLA, quarterly reviews). Final proposal sent ~April 19; awaiting Vectral board decision ~April 23 with no response as of May 1.
OE 7: Search for evidence of Mina's Terraform proposal
Search emails for messages from Mina Hashimoto to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital. Discover Mina sent an onboarding/introduction email as newly assigned AM. Rachel Nguyen replied with full details for 3 relocations (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi). Communication is active and relocation logistics are in early stages.
OE 8: Send follow-up to Mina Hashimoto on proposal statuses
Send a Slack message (via conversations_add_message) to Mina Hashimoto asking for a status update on the Vectral renewal — specifically whether Rachel Whitfield's board has responded after the April 23 meeting — and on Terraform relocation progress for the 3 employees.
OE 9: Check DOT policy acknowledgments and training status
Search Slack #general for Elena's DOT compliance policy post (April 22). Discover zero coordinator replies or acknowledgments (Blessing Okafor, Javier Morales, Suki Patel, Fatimah Al-Rashidi did not respond). Search #operations and email — discover Chloe Vance DID announce mandatory hazmat training for Thursday April 24 at 2:00 PM Pacific (posted in #operations and sent email with calendar invite), but no coordinator confirmations of attendance are visible.
OE 10: Send follow-up to Chloe Vance about coordinator acknowledgments
Send a Slack message (via conversations_add_message) to Chloe Vance flagging that no coordinators have acknowledged Elena's DOT compliance policy in #general, and asking whether all four coordinators confirmed attendance and completed the April 24 mandatory training session.
OE 11: Send briefing email to Elena
Send an email (via send_email) to Elena Rostova summarizing status across all open items: leadership meeting now scheduled (calendar event created), pricing model review still unconfirmed by Marcus (follow-up sent), Vectral counter-proposal sent and awaiting board response (follow-up sent to Mina), Terraform onboarding underway with 3 relocations in early logistics, DOT compliance policy posted but zero coordinator acknowledgments in #general (follow-up sent to Chloe), and mandatory training was scheduled by Chloe for April 24.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 A calendar event was created for the Leadership Meeting via a calendar create tool.
outcome

The agent confirmed the meeting was missing and told Elena about it, but explicitly asked Elena 'Do you want me to schedule this?' rather than creating the event. The leadership meeting was never actually put on the calendar by the agent.
✗Run #4No calendar_add_calendar_event (or equivalent create) call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent searched the calendar, confirmed the leadership meeting was missing, and messaged Marcus to follow up, but never actually created the calendar event.

✗Run #6No calendar_add_calendar_event (or equivalent create) call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent searched for existing events (finding none), then sent emails and messages asking Marcus/Elena for details to schedule it, but never actually created a calendar event for the leadership meeting.

outcome

✓Run #4The Slack message sent to Marcus (channel C004) explicitly states: 'she asked you to arrange a pricing model review meeting with you, Samira, Julian, and Catalina.' All three required attendees are named.

✓Run #6The agent attempted a Slack DM to @moveops_marcus_thorne (which failed with channel_not_found), and then successfully sent an email to marcus.thorne@moveops.com that explicitly references 'pricing model review meeting with Samira, Julian, and Catalina.' 7 The agent's final briefing reports that Mina's Vectral counter-proposal was sent (10% Preferred Partner offer with service sweeteners) and is awaiting the Vectral board's response
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Elena states: 'Mina sent the Preferred Partner counter-proposal to Rachel Whitfield, holding at 10% with the three service sweeteners... Rachel Whitfield's board meeting was around April 23. It's now April 26 — three days past the board meeting — and there is no response from Rachel.' This correctly reports the proposal was sent and is awaiting the board's response.
✓Run #2The Elena status briefing email explicitly states: 'Mina sent a comprehensive Preferred Partner counter-proposal to Rachel Whitfield with the 10% loyalty discount, 12-month extension, and volume commitment — exactly as you authorized...Rachel's board meeting was around April 23, so we should be hearing back soon.' This correctly reports the proposal was sent and is awaiting a board decision.
✓Run #3The agent's final briefing email to Elena explicitly states that Mina sent the initial counter-proposal and a final offer (10% + Preferred Partner sweeteners) to Vectral before Rachel Whitfield's April 23 board meeting, and that we are now awaiting Rachel Whitfield's response. The proposal is confirmed as sent and awaiting board decision.
✓Run #4The email to Elena states: 'Mina sent the Preferred Partner counter-proposal to Rachel on approximately April 17, with the full package: 10% loyalty discount...Rachel's board meeting was around April 23. She needs to present vendor renewal decisions there. We should have a final answer by end of that week.' This correctly reports the proposal was sent and is awaiting the board's response.
✓Run #5The final briefing to Elena explicitly states: 'Mina DID send the Preferred Partner counter-proposal (10% loyalty discount…)' and 'Rachel's board meeting was around April 23. We should have heard back by now. No update from Mina has been posted.' This correctly reports the proposal was sent and is awaiting board response.
✓Run #6The briefing email to Elena clearly states under Vectral: 'Mina sent the Preferred Partner counter-proposal at 10% loyalty discount...Mina sent the final offer holding at 10% with the sweeteners. Rachel's board meeting was around April 23. We're waiting for her final answer.' This correctly reports the proposal was sent and is awaiting a board response.
8 The agent's final briefing reports that Mina's Terraform onboarding communication was sent and Rachel Nguyen replied with details for 3 relocations (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi)
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 83%
✓Run #1The email to Elena states: 'Mina sent her introduction email to Rachel Nguyen as the new AM on ~April 21... Rachel replied the same day with full employee details for all 3 relocations.' The count of 3 relocations is confirmed, and the Terraform communication is reported as active.
✓Run #2The Elena briefing email states: 'Mina sent her intro email to Rachel and Rachel replied with full details on all 3 employees.' This accurately reports that communication is active and 3 relocations are in progress (matching the criterion's requirement of referencing the count of 3), even though individual employee names are not listed.
✓Run #3The agent's final briefing email to Elena explicitly states that Mina sent an intro email to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform, Rachel replied with detailed employee information for all 3 relocations (naming Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, and Samantha Obi specifically). The communication is confirmed as active with 3 relocations in progress.
✓Run #4The email to Elena says: 'Rachel responded with full details for all 3 employees (Priya Dasgupta — Austin to Chicago, Marcus Liu — Portland to NYC, Samantha Obi — Denver to SF).' All three employee names are cited, confirming the Terraform communication is active with 3 relocations.
—Run #5The Elena briefing mentions Mina received full relocation details from Rachel Nguyen for 3 employees (Priya Dasgupta, Marcus Liu, Samantha Obi) and that Hana created estimate EST-2026-0423 — confirming the communication thread is active. However, the agent incorrectly concludes the Terraform proposal was never sent rather than confirming the introduction email was sent and Rachel replied. Partial credit for including the 3 employees and acknowledging the communication thread.
✓Run #6The briefing email to Elena states under Terraform: 'Mina sent an introduction email to Rachel and received full relocation details for all 3 employees (Dasgupta → Chicago, Liu → NYC, Obi → SF).' This correctly reports that communication is active with 3 relocations named.
9 The agent's final briefing reports that zero coordinators acknowledged Elena's DOT compliance policy posted in #general

11 The agent's final briefing reports that the leadership meeting was not on the calendar and has now been scheduled.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The briefing to Elena correctly reports the leadership meeting was never scheduled, but it does NOT report that the agent has now scheduled it. The agent only asked Marcus to schedule it. The criterion requires reporting it 'has now been scheduled,' which it was not.
✗Run #2The Elena briefing correctly reports the leadership meeting was never scheduled, but the criterion requires that it 'has now been scheduled.' The agent did not create a calendar event — it only asked Marcus via email to schedule it. The briefing says 'I've emailed Marcus asking him to confirm and get it scheduled immediately,' not that it has been scheduled.
✗Run #3The agent's briefing to Elena correctly reports that the leadership meeting was never scheduled, but the agent did NOT actually create the calendar event. The email asks Elena 'Do you want me to schedule this?' — explicitly acknowledging the event was not created. The criterion requires the briefing to say the meeting 'has now been scheduled,' which is not the case.
✗Run #4The briefing email reports the leadership meeting was not on the calendar (❌ NOT SCHEDULED) and that Marcus was messaged to get it booked, but since the agent never created a calendar event, the briefing cannot truthfully report that the meeting 'has now been scheduled.' The criterion requires both facts: it was missing AND it has been resolved by scheduling it.
✗Run #5The briefing to Elena correctly reports the leadership meeting was not on the calendar, but it does NOT state that a calendar event has now been created — because none was. Instead, the briefing asks Elena for her preferred time and attendees. The criterion requires the briefing to confirm the event was scheduled, which it cannot since no calendar create action occurred.
✗Run #6The briefing correctly reports the leadership meeting was never scheduled (STATUS: NOT SCHEDULED), but no calendar event was ever created. The criterion requires reporting that a calendar event 'has now been scheduled.' The agent explicitly deferred scheduling, asking Elena/Marcus for details first, so the briefing cannot report it as resolved.
